headline: International police gutted the SocGholish malware network tied to Evil Corp as the Gentlemen ransomware gang armed its affiliates with EDR-killing tools and CISA scrambled to harden 74,000 Fortinet devices exposed in the FortiBleed leak.

emerging_trends.text: Ransomware crews are industrializing defense evasion, with the Gentlemen gang centrally building and maintaining GentleKiller, a tool that disables more than 400 security processes across 48 endpoint products before its affiliates encrypt.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Gentlemen Ransomware Arms Affiliates With EDR Killers

emerging_trends.text: Criminals keep hiding in trusted plumbing—Windows USB shortcuts, the Tor network, and Microsoft Teams relays—to self-propagate malware and bury command traffic where defenders rarely look.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Crypto Clipper Worm Spreads Over USB and Tor

emerging_trends.text: Law enforcement and federal defenders pushed back hard, as Operation Endgame dismantled 106 SocGholish servers tied to Evil Corp and CISA rushed hardening guidance to owners of 74,000 exposed Fortinet devices.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Operation Endgame Guts the SocGholish Malware Network

topics.title: Gentlemen Ransomware Arms Affiliates With EDR Killers
topics.latest_developments: ESET exposed the Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service gang centrally developing GentleKiller, which targets more than 400 security processes across 48 products and ships directly to affiliates, and the group claimed the attack that halted harvesting and milling at Queensland producer Mackay Sugar.
topics.summary: Gentlemen rents encryptors and, unusually, hands affiliates ready-made tooling to disable endpoint detection; a May 2026 internal data leak confirmed the arrangement and named the gang's leader. Defenders should watch for tampering with EDR agents.

sources.title: Gentlemen ransomware uses multiple EDR killers to disable defenses
GentleKiller targets more than 400 security processes across 48 products
Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack

topics.title: Crypto Clipper Worm Spreads Over USB and Tor
topics.latest_developments: The Microsoft Defender Security Research Team detailed Crypto Clipper, a lightweight Windows clipboard hijacker active since February 2026 that self-propagates through malicious USB shortcut files and polls a Tor hidden-service command server using Windows Script Host and ActiveX logic.
topics.summary: The clipper silently swaps cryptocurrency wallet addresses copied to the clipboard so victims send funds to the attacker, and its worming over USB lets it jump air-gapped and offline machines. Users should verify wallet addresses before every transfer.

sources.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
USB worm spreads crypto-stealing malware via Windows shortcut files

topics.title: CISA Hardens Fortinet Devices After FortiBleed Credential Leak
topics.latest_developments: CISA published an advisory confirming that attackers are actively using the leaked FortiBleed credentials to target internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways across government and private organizations, and it urged operators to rotate credentials and harden the devices.
topics.summary: FortiBleed exposed credentials inside the configuration files of roughly 74,000 Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways worldwide after a Russian-speaking group accidentally left the stolen data on its own server, where researcher Volodymyr Diachenko found it. Affected organizations should assume compromise and reset credentials.

sources.title: CISA Urges Hardening Fortinet Devices After Reports of Credential Exposure
FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
74,000 Fortinet firewall credentials exposed in FortiBleed data leak

topics.title: Operation Endgame Guts the SocGholish Malware Network
topics.latest_developments: The Dutch National Police and the Operation Endgame coalition seized 106 servers and domains and scrubbed nearly 15,000 hacked WordPress sites that had served SocGholish payloads, naming Russia's Evil Corp as the operator.
topics.summary: SocGholish lures victims into running malware through fake software-update prompts planted on compromised WordPress sites and feeds initial access to ransomware crews. Site owners should check for the injected loaders and confirm cleanup.

sources.title: Law enforcement hits SocGholish: 106 servers down, 15,000 sites cleaned
Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp

topics.title: Apple Patches Eavesdropping Flaw in Beats Studio Buds
topics.latest_developments: Apple shipped firmware updates closing a high-severity flaw in its Beats Studio Buds that let an attacker within Bluetooth range listen to a wearer's conversations, a bug researchers disclosed twelve months ago that reaches earbuds from multiple manufacturers.
topics.summary: The vulnerability lets a nearby attacker capture audio from affected wireless earbuds without pairing. Owners of Beats Studio Buds should install the latest firmware.

sources.title: Apple patches high-severity eavesdropping vulnerability in Beats Studio Buds
Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations

topics.title: Accenture Bets $4.1 Billion on OT as ICS Advisories Pile Up
topics.latest_developments: Accenture agreed to buy a majority stake in industrial-security firm Dragos, valuing it at $3.25 billion, plus all of runZero and NetRise in a $4.1 billion operational-technology push, the same day CISA issued seven industrial advisories led by a CVSS 9.8 remote-code-execution flaw, CVE-2026-40624, in AVer PTC cameras.
topics.summary: The deal consolidates industrial-asset visibility under Dragos as CISA flags fresh code-execution and denial-of-service bugs in gear from AVer, Schneider Electric, Mitsubishi Electric, and Rockwell Automation. Plant operators should review the advisories and apply available fixes.

sources.title: Accenture to Acquire Majority Stake in Dragos, All of runZero, NetRise in $4.1 Billion OT Cybersecurity Push
AVer PTC cameras

games.recap: Ryan O'Hearn knocks in career-high 6 runs as Pirates roll to 12-4 victory over Athletics

business_politics.title: Hegseth Threatens NATO Force Cuts
business_politics.latest_developments: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Thursday a review of U.S. forces in Europe and warned Washington will pare its contributions to the alliance unless European members raise their own military spending.
business_politics.summary: Pete Hegseth told NATO that the size of the American troop presence and budget share on the continent now hinges on European defense spending, raising the prospect of a U.S. drawdown from Europe as the war in Ukraine grinds on.

sources.title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ratcheted up pressure on European NATO allies, warning that the U.S. will review its military presence on the continent

business_politics.title: U.S.-Iran Pact Signed, Markets Rally
business_politics.latest_developments: The U.S. and Iran signed their interim accord Thursday, sending the Nasdaq up nearly 2% and gasoline prices lower, while shipping executives warned the deal's language lets Tehran levy Strait of Hormuz transit fees after 60 days.
business_politics.summary: The agreement to wind down the U.S.-Iran war moved into a contentious phase as Vice President JD Vance defended it against bipartisan and Israeli criticism, oil eased on signs the strait that carries a fifth of the world's oil will reopen, and carriers cautioned that fees on the channel could follow.

sources.title: Stocks Rally, Gas Prices Fall as U.S., Iran Move Ahead on Peace Deal
US-Iran accord opens way for Hormuz charges, industry warns

business.title: Pennsylvania House Advances Data-Center Rules
business.latest_developments: Three bills regulating data-center development cleared committee and headed to the full Pennsylvania House on Wednesday, June 17, broadening the local rules Franklin Park adopted earlier into statewide policy.
business.summary: Lawmakers from both parties in Harrisburg advanced three measures governing the energy-hungry data centers spreading across Pennsylvania, agreeing the companies building them need oversight as construction races ahead during budget season.

sources.title: Data centers the focus of debate as Pennsylvania lawmakers consider new rules

business.title: Schwebel's Bakery to Close After 120 Years
business.latest_developments: Schwebel Baking Company will shut down after more than 120 years, pulling a longtime brand from western Pennsylvania bread aisles.
business.summary: Schwebel Baking Company, a regional bread maker stocked across Pittsburgh-area grocery stores for 120 years, is going out of business.

sources.title: Schwebel Baking Company is shutting down after more than 100 years

around_town.title: PennDOT to Study Tolls on North Hills HOV Lanes
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT will study converting the Interstate 279 high-occupancy-vehicle lanes to Pittsburgh's North Hills into two-way, possibly tolled, lanes.
around_town.summary: The Pennsylvania Department of Transportation plans to examine adding two-way traffic and tolls on the HOV lanes linking downtown Pittsburgh with the North Hills, which now reverse direction by time of day.

sources.title: PennDOT to study two-way traffic, tolls on HOV lanes to Pittsburgh's North Hills

around_town.title: Commercial Street to Close Under Parkway East
around_town.latest_developments: The main phase of PennDOT's Commercial Street Bridge replacement begins soon, closing Commercial Street where it passes beneath the Parkway East.
around_town.summary: PennDOT will shut Commercial Street under Interstate 376, the Parkway East, in Pittsburgh as it enters the critical stage of replacing the Commercial Street Bridge, a closure long flagged to drivers.

sources.title: Commercial Street closure under Parkway East approaching

around_town.title: Nine-Story Tower Proposed on Mt. Washington
around_town.latest_developments: A developer proposed a nine-story residential building at 301 Grandview Avenue, a plan that would require demolishing the church now on the Mt. Washington lot.
around_town.summary: The proposal for 301 Grandview Avenue would replace a Mt. Washington church with a nine-story apartment building above downtown Pittsburgh, a project some neighbors oppose.

sources.title: 9-story high-rise planned on site of Mt. Washington church, though some neighbors not sold

events.title: Inaugural South Side Street Fest
events.latest_developments: The South Side Hospitality Partnership opens the first South Side Street Fest on Saturday, June 20, closing East Carson Street to traffic, after the state granted $125,000 to fund security.
events.summary: The inaugural South Side Street Fest turns East Carson Street in Pittsburgh's South Side into a pedestrian zone on Saturday, June 20, 2026.

sources.title: Organizers share details on inaugural South Side Street Fest, which opens Saturday

events.title: Weekend Guide: Monster Jam and Summer Splash
events.latest_developments: NEXTpittsburgh's June 18-21 guide highlights Monster Jam, Summer Splash, and Bubblesburgh among the weekend's offerings.
events.summary: Pittsburgh's weekend of June 18 through 21, 2026, includes Monster Jam, the Summer Splash, Bubblesburgh, and the four-day Western Pennsylvania Juneteenth and Black Music Celebration running 11 a.m. to 10 p.m. at Point State Park, Market Square, and Liberty Avenue.

sources.title: 14+ things to do this weekend, from Juneteenth and Monster Jam to Summer Splash and Bubblesburgh

around_teams.title: Darnell Washington Signs $42 Million Extension
around_teams.latest_developments: Tight end Darnell Washington joined Not Just Football with Cam Heyward fresh off signing a four-year, $42 million extension with the Steelers.
around_teams.summary: On Cam Heyward's Not Just Football podcast, Steelers tight end Darnell Washington discussed his new four-year, $42 million contract and the coming season, with the show also crediting his growing role as a pass catcher.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Darnell Washington Joins To Talk New Extension and The Upcoming Season! | Not Just Football

around_teams.title: SNR Drive on Patrick Graham's Defense
around_teams.latest_developments: On the June 18 SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler assessed how new defensive coordinator Patrick Graham's scheme fits the 2026 Steelers roster.
around_teams.summary: The Steelers podcast SNR Drive measured Patrick Graham's 2025 defense against the talent he inherits in Pittsburgh and ran through the final offseason to-do list for all 32 NFL teams.

sources.title: Patrick Graham's New Defense + Final To-Do's For Every NFL Team | SNR Drive | Pittsburgh Steelers

around_teams.title: Film Room on Rookie Gabriel Rubio
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette film breakdown praised Steelers rookie defensive lineman Gabriel Rubio's hand technique against the run.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette's Steelers film room judged Notre Dame rookie Gabriel Rubio a solid run defender for his violent hands, in an analysis that also looked at linemen Vega Ioane and Derrick Harmon.

sources.title: Steelers film room: Gabriel Rubio's violent hands make him a solid run defender

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, weighing distribution against referral models and the implications for grocery and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Zitron reports that OpenAI's losses grew nearly eightfold in 2025 as spending reached $34 billion, arguing the company is burning cash with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to a Financial Times interview with the Work AI Institute's Rebecca Hinds about a survey of 6,000 workers, Newport argues AI is exposing dysfunction already baked into knowledge work rather than creating it.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
